bytecode.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /***************************************************************************
00002  *   Copyright © 2005-2011 by Gabriele Svelto                              *
00003  *   gabriele.svelto@gmail.com                                             *
00004  *                                                                         *
00005  *   This file is part of Jelatine.                                        *
00006  *                                                                         *
00007  *   Jelatine is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify      *
00008  *   it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by  *
00009  *   the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or     *
00010  *   (at your option) any later version.                                   *
00011  *                                                                         *
00012  *   Jelatine is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,           *
00013  *   but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of        *
00014  *   MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the         *
00015  *   GNU General Public License for more details.                          *
00016  *                                                                         *
00017  *   You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License     *
00018  *   along with Jelatine.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.     *
00019  ***************************************************************************/
00020 
00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "bytecode.h"
00027 #include "class.h"
00028 #include "constantpool.h"
00029 #include "loader.h"
00030 #include "memory.h"
00031 #include "method.h"
00032 #include "opcodes.h"
00033 #include "util.h"
00034 
00048 void translate_bytecode(class_t *cl, method_t *method, uint8_t *code,
00049                         exception_handler_t *handlers)
00050 {
00051     const_pool_t *cp = cl->const_pool;
00052     uint8_t opcode;
00053     int32_t i = 0;
00054     int32_t j = 0;
00055     int32_t code_length = method_get_code_length(method);
00056     bool synchronized = method_is_synchronized(method);
00057 
00058     /* Add a special monitor enter opcode at the beginning of the code if the
00059      * method is synchronized */
00060     if (synchronized) {
00061         if (method_is_static(method)) {
00062             code[0] = MONITORENTER_SPECIAL_STATIC;
00063         } else {
00064             code[0] = MONITORENTER_SPECIAL;
00065         }
00066 
00067         i++;
00068     }
00069 
00070     while  (i < code_length) {
00071         opcode = code[i];
00072 
00073         switch (opcode) {
00074             // Leave the following opcodes unchanged
00075             case JAVA_NOP: // NOP
00076             case JAVA_ACONST_NULL: // ACONST_NULL
00077             case JAVA_ICONST_M1: // ICONST_M1
00078             case JAVA_ICONST_0: // ICONST_0
00079             case JAVA_ICONST_1: // ICONST_1
00080             case JAVA_ICONST_2: // ICONST_2
00081             case JAVA_ICONST_3: // ICONST_3
00082             case JAVA_ICONST_4: // ICONST_4
00083             case JAVA_ICONST_5: // ICONST_5
00084             case JAVA_LCONST_0: // LCONST_0
00085             case JAVA_LCONST_1: // LCONST_1
00086 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00087             case JAVA_FCONST_0: // FCONST_0
00088             case JAVA_FCONST_1: // FCONST_1
00089             case JAVA_FCONST_2: // FCONST_2
00090             case JAVA_DCONST_0: // DCONST_0
00091             case JAVA_DCONST_1: // DCONST_1
00092 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00093                 i++;
00094                 break;
00095 
00096             case JAVA_BIPUSH: // BIPUSH
00097                 i += 2;
00098                 break;
00099 
00100             case JAVA_SIPUSH: // SIPUSH
00101             {
00102                 // Turn the number in the native endian format
00103                 int16_t temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00104                 store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00105                 i += 3;
00106             }
00107                 break;
00108 
00109             case JAVA_LDC:
00110             {
00111                 uint8_t tag;
00112 
00113                 /* Translate to LDC_STRING if used to load a string reference,
00114                  * this will also throw an exception if the constant pool index
00115                  * is out of bounds */
00116                 tag = cp_get_tag(cp, code[i + 1]);
00117 
00118                 if ((tag == CONSTANT_String) || (tag == CONSTANT_Class)) {
00119                     code[i] = LDC_PRELINK;
00120                 } else {
00121                     code[i] = LDC;
00122 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00123                     if (tag != CONSTANT_Float && tag != CONSTANT_Integer) {
00124                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00125                                 "LDC instruction constant pool index refers to "
00126                                 "an element which is neither CONSTANT_String "
00127                                 "CONSTANT_Integer nor CONSTANT_Float");
00128                     }
00129 #else
00130                     if (tag != CONSTANT_Integer) {
00131                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00132                                 "LDC instruction constant pool index refers to "
00133                                 "an element which is neither CONSTANT_String "
00134                                 "nor CONSTANT_Integer");
00135                     }
00136 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00137                 }
00138 
00139                 i += 2;
00140             }
00141                 break;
00142 
00143             case JAVA_LDC_W:
00144             {
00145                 /* Translate to LDC_W_STRING if used to load a string reference,
00146                  * this will also throw an exception if the constant pool index
00147                  * is out of bounds */
00148                 uint16_t temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00149                 uint8_t tag = cp_get_tag(cp, temp);
00150 
00151                 if ((tag == CONSTANT_String) || (tag == CONSTANT_Class)) {
00152                     code[i] = LDC_W_PRELINK;
00153                 } else {
00154                     code[i] = LDC_W;
00155                     store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00156 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00157                     if (tag != CONSTANT_Float && tag != CONSTANT_Integer) {
00158                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00159                                 "LDC_W instruction constant pool index refers "
00160                                 "to an element which is neither CONSTANT_String"
00161                                 ", CONSTANT_Integer nor CONSTANT_Float");
00162                     }
00163 #else
00164                     if (tag != CONSTANT_Integer) {
00165                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00166                                 "LDC_W instruction constant pool index refers "
00167                                 "to an element which is neither CONSTANT_String"
00168                                 " nor CONSTANT_Integer");
00169                     }
00170 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00171                 }
00172 
00173                 i += 3;
00174             }
00175                 break;
00176 
00177             case JAVA_LDC2_W:
00178             {
00179                 uint16_t temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00180                 uint8_t tag = cp_get_tag(cp, temp);
00181 
00182 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00183                 if (tag != CONSTANT_Double && tag != CONSTANT_Long) {
00184                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00185                             "LDC2_W instruction constant pool index refers to "
00186                             "an element which is neither CONSTANT_Double nor "
00187                             "CONSTANT_Long");
00188                 }
00189 #else
00190                 if (tag != CONSTANT_Long) {
00191                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00192                             "LDC2_W instruction constant pool index refers to "
00193                             "an element which is not CONSTANT_Long");
00194                 }
00195 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00196 
00197                 store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00198                 i += 3;
00199             }
00200                 break;
00201 
00202             case JAVA_ILOAD: // ILOAD
00203             case JAVA_LLOAD: // LLOAD
00204 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00205             case JAVA_FLOAD: // FLOAD
00206             case JAVA_DLOAD: // DLOAD
00207 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00208             case JAVA_ALOAD: // ALOAD
00209             case JAVA_ISTORE: // ISTORE
00210             case JAVA_LSTORE: // LSTORE
00211 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00212             case JAVA_FSTORE: // FSTORE
00213             case JAVA_DSTORE: // DSTORE
00214 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00215             case JAVA_ASTORE: // ASTORE
00216             {
00217                 uint8_t temp = code[i + 1];
00218 
00219                 if (temp > method->max_locals - 1) {
00220                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00221                             "ILOAD, FLOAD, ALOAD, LLOAD, DLOAD, ISTORE, FSTORE,"
00222                             " ASTORE, LSTORE or DSTORE accesses a non-existing "
00223                             "local variable");
00224                 }
00225 
00226                 i += 2;
00227             }
00228                 break;
00229 
00230             case JAVA_ILOAD_0: // ILOAD_0
00231             case JAVA_LLOAD_0: // LLOAD_0
00232 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00233             case JAVA_FLOAD_0: // FLOAD_0
00234             case JAVA_DLOAD_0: // DLOAD_0
00235 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00236             case JAVA_ALOAD_0: // ALOAD_0
00237             case JAVA_ISTORE_0: // ISTORE_0
00238             case JAVA_LSTORE_0: // LSTORE_0
00239 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00240             case JAVA_FSTORE_0: // FSTORE_0
00241             case JAVA_DSTORE_0: // DSTORE_0
00242 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00243             case JAVA_ASTORE_0: // ASTORE_0
00244                 if (method->max_locals == 0) {
00245                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00246                             "ILOAD_0, FLOAD_0, ALOAD_0, LLOAD_0, DLOAD_0, "
00247                             "ISTORE_0, LSTORE_0, FSTORE_0, DSTORE_0 or ASTORE_0"
00248                             " accesses a non-existing local variable");
00249                 }
00250 
00251                 i++;
00252                 break;
00253 
00254             case JAVA_ILOAD_1: // ILOAD_1
00255             case JAVA_LLOAD_1: // LLOAD_1
00256 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00257             case JAVA_FLOAD_1: // FLOAD_1
00258             case JAVA_DLOAD_1: // DLOAD_1
00259 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00260             case JAVA_ALOAD_1: // ALOAD_1
00261             case JAVA_ISTORE_1: // ISTORE_1
00262             case JAVA_LSTORE_1: // LSTORE_1
00263 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00264             case JAVA_FSTORE_1: // FSTORE_1
00265             case JAVA_DSTORE_1: // DSTORE_1
00266 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00267             case JAVA_ASTORE_1: // ASTORE_1
00268                 if (method->max_locals < 2) {
00269                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00270                             "ILOAD_1, FLOAD_1, ALOAD_1, LLOAD_1, DLOAD_1, "
00271                             "ISTORE_1, LSTORE_1, FSTORE_1, DSTORE_1 or ASTORE_1"
00272                             " accesses a non-existing local variable");
00273                 }
00274 
00275                 i++;
00276                 break;
00277 
00278             case JAVA_ILOAD_2: // ILOAD_2
00279             case JAVA_LLOAD_2: // LLOAD_2
00280 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00281             case JAVA_FLOAD_2: // FLOAD_2
00282             case JAVA_DLOAD_2: // DLOAD_2
00283 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00284             case JAVA_ALOAD_2: // ALOAD_2
00285             case JAVA_ISTORE_2: // ISTORE_2
00286             case JAVA_LSTORE_2: // LSTORE_2
00287 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00288             case JAVA_FSTORE_2: // FSTORE_2
00289             case JAVA_DSTORE_2: // DSTORE_2
00290 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00291             case JAVA_ASTORE_2: // ASTORE_2
00292                 if (method->max_locals < 3) {
00293                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00294                             "ILOAD_2, FLOAD_2, ALOAD_2, LLOAD_2, DLOAD_2, "
00295                             "ISTORE_2, LSTORE_2, FSTORE_2, DSTORE_2 or ASTORE_2"
00296                             " accesses a non-existing local variable");
00297                 }
00298 
00299                 i++;
00300                 break;
00301 
00302             case JAVA_ILOAD_3: // ILOAD_3
00303             case JAVA_LLOAD_3: // LLOAD_3
00304 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00305             case JAVA_FLOAD_3: // FLOAD_3
00306             case JAVA_DLOAD_3: // DLOAD_3
00307 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00308             case JAVA_ALOAD_3: // ALOAD_3
00309             case JAVA_ISTORE_3: // ISTORE_3
00310             case JAVA_LSTORE_3: // LSTORE_3
00311 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00312             case JAVA_FSTORE_3: // FSTORE_3
00313             case JAVA_DSTORE_3: // DSTORE_3
00314 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00315             case JAVA_ASTORE_3: // ASTORE_3
00316                 if (method->max_locals < 4) {
00317                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00318                             "ILOAD_3, FLOAD_3, ALOAD_3, LLOAD_3, DLOAD_3, "
00319                             "ISTORE_3, LSTORE_3, FSTORE_3, DSTORE_3 or ASTORE_3"
00320                             " accesses a non-existing local variable");
00321                 }
00322 
00323                 i++;
00324                 break;
00325 
00326             case JAVA_IALOAD: // IALOAD
00327             case JAVA_LALOAD: // LALOAD
00328 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00329             case JAVA_FALOAD: // FALOAD
00330             case JAVA_DALOAD: // DALOAD
00331 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00332             case JAVA_AALOAD: // AALOAD
00333             case JAVA_BALOAD: // BALOAD
00334             case JAVA_CALOAD: // CALOAD
00335             case JAVA_SALOAD: // SALOAD
00336             case JAVA_IASTORE: // IASTORE
00337             case JAVA_LASTORE: // LASTORE
00338 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00339             case JAVA_FASTORE: // FASTORE
00340             case JAVA_DASTORE: // DASTORE
00341 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00342             case JAVA_AASTORE: // AASTORE
00343             case JAVA_BASTORE: // BASTORE
00344             case JAVA_CASTORE: // CASTORE
00345             case JAVA_SASTORE: // SASTORE
00346             case JAVA_POP: // POP
00347             case JAVA_POP2: // POP2
00348             case JAVA_DUP: // DUP
00349             case JAVA_DUP_X1: // DUP_X1
00350             case JAVA_DUP_X2: // DUP_X2
00351             case JAVA_DUP2: // DUP2
00352             case JAVA_DUP2_X1: // DUP2_X1
00353             case JAVA_DUP2_X2: // DUP2_X2
00354             case JAVA_SWAP: // SWAP
00355             case JAVA_IADD: // IADD
00356             case JAVA_LADD: // LADD
00357 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00358             case JAVA_FADD: // FADD
00359             case JAVA_DADD: // DADD
00360 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00361             case JAVA_ISUB: // ISUB
00362             case JAVA_LSUB: // LSUB
00363 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00364             case JAVA_FSUB: // FSUB
00365             case JAVA_DSUB: // DSUB
00366 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00367             case JAVA_IMUL: // IMUL
00368             case JAVA_LMUL: // LMUL
00369 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00370             case JAVA_FMUL: // FMUL
00371             case JAVA_DMUL: // DMUL
00372 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00373             case JAVA_IDIV: // IDIV
00374             case JAVA_LDIV: // LDIV
00375 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00376             case JAVA_FDIV: // FDIV
00377             case JAVA_DDIV: // DDIV
00378 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00379             case JAVA_IREM: // IREM
00380             case JAVA_LREM: // LREM
00381 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00382             case JAVA_FREM: // FREM
00383             case JAVA_DREM: // DREM
00384 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00385             case JAVA_INEG: // INEG
00386             case JAVA_LNEG: // LNEG
00387 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00388             case JAVA_FNEG: // FNEG
00389             case JAVA_DNEG: // DNEG
00390 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00391             case JAVA_ISHL: // ISHL
00392             case JAVA_LSHL: // LSHL
00393             case JAVA_ISHR: // ISHR
00394             case JAVA_LSHR: // LSHR
00395             case JAVA_IUSHR: // IUSHR
00396             case JAVA_LUSHR: // LUSHR
00397             case JAVA_IAND: // IAND
00398             case JAVA_LAND: // LAND
00399             case JAVA_IOR: // IOR
00400             case JAVA_LOR: // LOR
00401             case JAVA_IXOR: // IXOR
00402             case JAVA_LXOR: // LXOR
00403                 i++;
00404                 break;
00405 
00406             case JAVA_IINC: // IINC
00407                 if (code[i + 1] > method->max_locals - 1) {
00408                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00409                             "IINC accesses a non-existing local variable");
00410                 }
00411 
00412                 i += 3;
00413                 break;
00414 
00415             case JAVA_I2L: // I2L
00416 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00417             case JAVA_I2F: // I2F
00418             case JAVA_I2D:
00419 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00420             case JAVA_L2I: // L2I
00421 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00422             case JAVA_L2F: // L2F
00423             case JAVA_L2D: // L2D
00424             case JAVA_F2I: // F2I
00425             case JAVA_F2L: // F2L
00426             case JAVA_F2D: // F2D
00427             case JAVA_D2I: // D2I
00428             case JAVA_D2L: // D2L
00429             case JAVA_D2F: // D2F
00430 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00431             case JAVA_I2B: // I2B
00432             case JAVA_I2C: // I2C
00433             case JAVA_I2S: // I2S
00434             case JAVA_LCMP: // LCMP
00435 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00436             case JAVA_FCMPL: // FCMPL
00437             case JAVA_FCMPG: // FCMPG
00438             case JAVA_DCMPL: // DCMPL
00439             case JAVA_DCMPG: // DCMPG
00440 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00441                 i++;
00442                 break;
00443 
00444             case JAVA_IFEQ: // IFEW
00445             case JAVA_IFNE: // IFNE
00446             case JAVA_IFLT: // IFLT
00447             case JAVA_IFGE: // IFGE
00448             case JAVA_IFGT: // IFGT
00449             case JAVA_IFLE: // IFLE
00450             case JAVA_IF_ICMPEQ: // IF_ICMPEQ
00451             case JAVA_IF_ICMPNE: // IF_ICMPNE
00452             case JAVA_IF_ICMPLT: // IF_ICMPLT
00453             case JAVA_IF_ICMPGE: // IF_ICMPGE
00454             case JAVA_IF_ICMPGT: // IF_CMPGT
00455             case JAVA_IF_ICMPLE: // IF_CMPLE
00456             case JAVA_IF_ACMPEQ: // IF_ACMPEQ
00457             case JAVA_IF_ACMPNE: // IC_ACMPNE
00458             case JAVA_GOTO: // GOTO
00459             {
00460                 int16_t temp;
00461 
00462                 temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00463 
00464                 if ((i + temp > code_length - 1) || (i + temp < 0)) {
00465                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00466                             "Jump instruction address outside of the code "
00467                             "range");
00468                 }
00469 
00470                 store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00471                 i += 3;
00472             }
00473                 break;
00474 
00475             case JAVA_JSR:
00476             case JAVA_RET:
00477                 // This opcodes shouldn't be present in preverified bytecode
00478                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00479                         "JSR or RET opcode found");
00480                 break;
00481 
00482             case JAVA_TABLESWITCH: // TABLESWITCH
00483             {
00484                 /*
00485                  * The integers used by the table-switch instruction are loaded
00486                  * and turned automagically in the machines native endian format
00487                  * so that they can be loaded safely as 4-byte integers later,
00488                  * natural alignment of those integers is guaranteed by the
00489                  * bytecode
00490                  */
00491                 int32_t temp;
00492                 int32_t low;
00493                 int32_t high;
00494                 int32_t base;
00495                 int32_t *aligned_ptr;
00496 
00497                 base = i;
00498                 i = size_ceil(i + 1, 4);
00499                 aligned_ptr = (int32_t *) (code + i);
00500 
00501                 temp = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00502                        | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00503 
00504                 if ((base + temp > code_length - 1) || (base + temp < 0)) {
00505                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00506                             "Default TABLESWITCH address outside of the code "
00507                             "range");
00508                 }
00509 
00510                 *aligned_ptr = temp;
00511                 aligned_ptr++;
00512                 i += 4;
00513 
00514                 low = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00515                       | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00516 
00517                 *aligned_ptr = low;
00518                 aligned_ptr++;
00519                 i += 4;
00520 
00521                 high = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00522                        | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00523 
00524                 *aligned_ptr = high;
00525                 aligned_ptr++;
00526                 i += 4;
00527 
00528                 if (high < low) {
00529                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00530                             "high < low in TABLESWITCH opcode");
00531                 }
00532 
00533                 j = 0;
00534 
00535                 while (j < (high - low + 1)) {
00536                     temp = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00537                            | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00538 
00539                     if ((base + temp > code_length - 1) || (base + temp < 0)) {
00540                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00541                                 "TABLESWITCH address outside of the code "
00542                                 "range");
00543                     }
00544 
00545                     *aligned_ptr = temp;
00546                     aligned_ptr++;
00547                     i += 4;
00548 
00549                     j++;
00550                 }
00551             }
00552                 break;
00553 
00554             case JAVA_LOOKUPSWITCH: // LOOKUPSWITCH
00555             {
00556                 /*
00557                  * The integers used by the lookup-switch instruction are loaded
00558                  * and turned automagically in the machines native endian format
00559                  * so that they can be loaded safely as 4-byte integers later,
00560                  * natural alignment of those integers is guaranteed by the
00561                  * bytecode
00562                  */
00563                 int32_t temp;
00564                 int32_t npairs;
00565                 int32_t match;
00566                 int32_t match_old;
00567                 int32_t offset;
00568                 int32_t base;
00569                 int32_t *aligned_ptr;
00570 
00571                 base = i;
00572                 match_old = 0;
00573                 i = size_ceil(i + 1, 4);
00574                 aligned_ptr = (int32_t *) (code + i);
00575 
00576                 temp = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00577                        | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00578 
00579                 if ((base + temp > code_length - 1) || (base + temp < 0)) {
00580                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00581                             "Default LOOKUPSWITCH address outside of the code "
00582                             "range");
00583                 }
00584 
00585                 *aligned_ptr = temp;
00586                 aligned_ptr++;
00587                 i += 4;
00588 
00589                 npairs = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00590                          | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00591 
00592                 if (npairs < 0) {
00593                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00594                             "npairs < 0 in LOOKUPSWITCH opcode");
00595                 }
00596 
00597                 *aligned_ptr = npairs;
00598                 aligned_ptr++;
00599                 i += 4;
00600                 j = 0;
00601 
00602                 while (j < npairs) {
00603                     match = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00604                             | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00605 
00606                     *aligned_ptr = match;
00607                     aligned_ptr++;
00608                     i += 4;
00609 
00610                     offset = (code[i] << 24) | (code[i + 1] << 16)
00611                              | (code[i + 2] << 8) | code[i + 3];
00612 
00613                     if ((base + offset > code_length - 1)
00614                         || (base + offset < 0))
00615                     {
00616                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00617                                 "LOOKUPSWITCH address outside of the code "
00618                                 "range");
00619                     }
00620 
00621                     *aligned_ptr = offset;
00622                     aligned_ptr++;
00623                     i += 4;
00624 
00625                     if (j > 0 && match <= match_old) {
00626                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00627                                 "Unordered match keys in LOOKUPSWITCH opcode");
00628                     }
00629 
00630                     match_old = match;
00631                     j++;
00632                 }
00633             }
00634                 break;
00635 
00636             case JAVA_IRETURN: // IRETURN
00637                 if (synchronized) {
00638                     code[i] = IRETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00639                 }
00640 
00641                 i++;
00642                 break;
00643 
00644             case JAVA_LRETURN: // LRETURN
00645                 if (synchronized) {
00646                     code[i] = LRETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00647                 }
00648 
00649                 i++;
00650                 break;
00651 
00652 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00653 
00654             case JAVA_FRETURN: // FRETURN
00655                 if (synchronized) {
00656                     code[i] = FRETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00657                 }
00658 
00659                 i++;
00660                 break;
00661 
00662             case JAVA_DRETURN: // DRETURN
00663                 if (synchronized) {
00664                     code[i] = DRETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00665                 }
00666 
00667                 i++;
00668                 break;
00669 
00670 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00671 
00672             case JAVA_ARETURN: // ARETURN
00673                 if (synchronized) {
00674                     code[i] = ARETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00675                 }
00676 
00677                 i++;
00678                 break;
00679 
00680             case JAVA_RETURN: // RETURN
00681                 if (synchronized) {
00682                     code[i] = RETURN_MONITOREXIT;
00683                 }
00684 
00685                 i++;
00686                 break;
00687 
00688             case JAVA_GETSTATIC: // GETSTATIC_PRELINK
00689             case JAVA_PUTSTATIC: // PUTSTATIC_PRELINK
00690             case JAVA_GETFIELD: // GETFIELD_PRELINK
00691             case JAVA_PUTFIELD: // PUTFIELD_PRELINK
00692                 i += 3;
00693                 break;
00694 
00695             case JAVA_INVOKEVIRTUAL:
00696                 code[i] = INVOKEVIRTUAL_PRELINK;
00697                 i += 3;
00698                 break;
00699 
00700             case JAVA_INVOKESPECIAL:
00701                 code[i] = INVOKESPECIAL_PRELINK;
00702                 i += 3;
00703                 break;
00704 
00705             case JAVA_INVOKESTATIC:
00706                 code[i] = INVOKESTATIC_PRELINK;
00707                 i += 3;
00708                 break;
00709 
00710             case JAVA_INVOKEINTERFACE:
00711                 code[i] = INVOKEINTERFACE_PRELINK;
00712 
00713                 if (code[i + 3] == 0) {
00714                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00715                             "The 'count' of an INVOKEINTERFACE opcode is zero");
00716                 }
00717 
00718                 /* RETURN opcodes will find two NOPs after an interface
00719                  * declaration, this is slower than an optimal implementation
00720                  * but still works */
00721                 code[i + 3] = NOP;
00722                 code[i + 4] = NOP;
00723                 i += 5;
00724                 break;
00725 
00726             case JAVA_NEW:
00727                 code[i] = NEW_PRELINK;
00728                 i += 3;
00729                 break;
00730 
00731             case JAVA_NEWARRAY:
00732                 code[i] = NEWARRAY_PRELINK;
00733 
00734                 switch (code[i + 1]) {
00735                     case T_BOOLEAN:
00736                     case T_CHAR:
00737 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00738                     case T_FLOAT:
00739                     case T_DOUBLE:
00740 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00741                     case T_BYTE:
00742                     case T_SHORT:
00743                     case T_INT:
00744                     case T_LONG:
00745                         break;
00746 
00747                     default:
00748 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00749                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00750                             "NEWARRAY operand is not of type T_BOOLEAN, T_CHAR,"
00751                             "T_FLOAT, T_DOUBLE, T_BYTE, T_SHORT, T_INT or "
00752                             "T_LONG");
00753 #else
00754                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00755                             "NEWARRAY operand is not of type T_BOOLEAN, T_CHAR,"
00756                             "T_BYTE, T_SHORT, T_INT or T_LONG");
00757 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00758                 }
00759 
00760                 i += 2;
00761                 break;
00762 
00763             case JAVA_ANEWARRAY:
00764                 code[i] = ANEWARRAY_PRELINK;
00765                 i += 3;
00766                 break;
00767 
00768             case JAVA_ARRAYLENGTH: // ARRAYLENGTH
00769             case JAVA_ATHROW: // ATHROW
00770                 i++;
00771                 break;
00772 
00773             case JAVA_CHECKCAST:
00774                 code[i] = CHECKCAST_PRELINK;
00775                 i += 3;
00776                 break;
00777 
00778             case JAVA_INSTANCEOF:
00779                 code[i] = INSTANCEOF_PRELINK;
00780                 i += 3;
00781                 break;
00782 
00783             case JAVA_MONITORENTER: // MONITORENTER
00784             case JAVA_MONITOREXIT: // MONITOREXIT
00785                 i++;
00786                 break;
00787 
00788             case JAVA_WIDE:
00789                 i++;
00790 
00791                 switch (code[i]) {
00792                     case JAVA_ILOAD:
00793                     case JAVA_LLOAD:
00794 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00795                     case JAVA_FLOAD:
00796                     case JAVA_DLOAD:
00797 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00798                     case JAVA_ALOAD:
00799                     case JAVA_ISTORE:
00800                     case JAVA_LSTORE:
00801 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00802                     case JAVA_FSTORE:
00803                     case JAVA_DSTORE:
00804 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00805                     case JAVA_ASTORE:
00806                     {
00807                         uint16_t temp;
00808 
00809                         temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00810 
00811                         if (temp > method->max_locals - 1) {
00812                             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00813                                     "WIDE ILOAD, FLOAD, ALOAD, LLOAD, DLOAD, "
00814                                     "ISTORE, FSTORE, ASTORE, LSTORE or DSTORE "
00815                                     "accesses a non-existing local variable");
00816                         }
00817 
00818                         store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00819                         i += 3;
00820                     }
00821                         break;
00822 
00823                     case JAVA_IINC:
00824                     {
00825                         uint16_t temp;
00826 
00827                         temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00828 
00829                         if (temp > method->max_locals - 1) {
00830                             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00831                                     "WIDE IINC accesses a non-existing local "
00832                                     "variable");
00833                         }
00834 
00835                         store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00836                         temp = (code[i + 3] << 8) | code[i + 4];
00837                         store_int16_un(code + i + 3, temp);
00838                         i += 5;
00839                     }
00840                         break;
00841 
00842                     default:
00843                         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00844                                 "Unknown or wrong opcode after WIDE opcode");
00845                 }
00846 
00847                 break;
00848 
00849             case JAVA_MULTIANEWARRAY:
00850                 code[i] = MULTIANEWARRAY_PRELINK;
00851 
00852                 if (code[i + 3] == 0) {
00853                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00854                             "MULTIANEWARRAY opcode has 0 dimensions count");
00855                 }
00856 
00857                 i += 4;
00858                 break;
00859 
00860             case JAVA_IFNULL: // IFNULL
00861             case JAVA_IFNONNULL: // IFNONNULL
00862             {
00863                 int16_t temp;
00864 
00865                 temp = (code[i + 1] << 8) | code[i + 2];
00866 
00867                 if ((i + temp > code_length - 1) || (i + temp < 0)) {
00868                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00869                             "Jump instruction address outside of the code "
00870                             "range");
00871                 }
00872 
00873                 store_int16_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00874                 i += 3;
00875             }
00876                 break;
00877 
00878             case JAVA_GOTO_W: // GOTO_W
00879             {
00880                 int32_t temp;
00881 
00882                 temp = (code[i + 1] << 24) | (code[i + 2] << 16)
00883                        | (code[i + 3] << 8) | code[i + 4];
00884 
00885                 if ((i + temp > code_length - 1) || (i + temp < 0)) {
00886                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00887                             "Jump instruction address outside of the code "
00888                             "range");
00889                 }
00890 
00891                 store_int32_un(code + i + 1, temp);
00892                 i += 5;
00893             }
00894                 break;
00895 
00896             case JAVA_JSR_W:
00897                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00898                         "JSR_W opcode found");
00899                 break;
00900 
00901             default:
00902                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00903                         "Unknown opcode found");
00904         }
00905     }
00906 
00907     if (i != code_length) {
00908         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00909                 "Last opcode in the code exceeds the specified length");
00910     }
00911 
00912     // Check consistency of the exception handlers
00913     for (i = 0; i < method->exception_table_length; i++) {
00914         if ((handlers[i].start_pc >= handlers[i].end_pc)
00915             || ((int32_t) handlers[i].start_pc >= code_length)
00916             || ((int32_t) handlers[i].end_pc > code_length))
00917         {
00918             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00919                     "Malformed exception handler");
00920         }
00921     }
00922 } // translate_bytecode()
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:38:44 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
